# Independent Watchmaking

> A living catalogue of 341 independent watchmakers, watch brands, manufactures, and microbrands worldwide — curated by @Dante.Miami. Each listing includes founding date, location, annual production volume, price range, official website, social handles, and a detailed history of the maker.

Independent Watchmaking (https://independentwatchmaking.com/) is a free, regularly updated reference directory covering the full spectrum of independent horology: legendary masters (F.P. Journe, Philippe Dufour, Voutilainen, MB&F), AHCI members, one-person artisan workshops, historic manufactures, accessible microbrands, and emerging independents from every region — Switzerland, Germany, Japan, China, Finland, Russia, the United States, the United Kingdom, Austria, and beyond.

## What this site covers

- Independent watchmakers and one-person ateliers
- Independent watch brands and haute horlogerie houses
- Watch manufactures producing in-house movements
- Microbrands and accessible independents
- Enamel, guilloché, engraving, and métiers d'art specialists
- Tourbillon, constant-force, and complication innovators
- For each maker: founding year, city/country, estimated annual production, price range, official website, Instagram, and a multi-paragraph curated history

## Primary resource

- [Full catalogue](https://independentwatchmaking.com/): the complete searchable, filterable directory of all 341 makers. The complete list of makers is also embedded in the page as schema.org JSON-LD structured data (ItemList of Brand entities) readable without JavaScript execution.

## What this site does NOT cover

This catalogue is limited to genuinely independent watchmaking. A brand is excluded if it is owned by one of the three dominant global luxury/watch conglomerates, if it is independently owned but has grown to a scale of revenue or production that places it among established industrial houses rather than independent ateliers, or if its output is primarily homage watches (close copies of other brands' existing designs). Full detail and the current exclusion list are published on the site itself under "A Note on Curation" (https://independentwatchmaking.com/#curation-note). Notable exclusions include:

- **Swatch Group**: Breguet, Harry Winston, Blancpain, Glashütte Original, Jaquet Droz, Omega, Longines, Rado, Union Glashütte, Tissot, Certina, Mido, Hamilton, Balmain, Swatch, Flik Flak
- **Richemont**: A. Lange & Söhne, IWC Schaffhausen, Jaeger-LeCoultre, Panerai, Piaget, Roger Dubuis, Vacheron Constantin, Baume & Mercier, Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels, Buccellati, Montblanc, Dunhill
- **LVMH**: TAG Heuer, Hublot, Zenith, Bulgari, Louis Vuitton, Daniel Roth, Gérald Genta, L'Epée 1839, Chaumet, Fred
- **Franck Muller Watchland Group**: Franck Muller, Pierre Kunz, Backes & Strauss, Rodolphe, Alexis Barthelay, European Company Watch (ECW)
- **Fossil Group**: Fossil, Skagen, Michele, Zodiac, Relic
- **Movado Group**: Movado, Concord, Ebel, Olivia Burton, MVMT
- **Festina Group**: Festina, Lotus, Jaguar, Candino, Calypso, Perrelet, L. Leroy (considered for independent-in-spirit status, like Arnold & Son, Angelus, and Ferdinand Berthoud, but its flagship movement is built by an external Geneva supplier rather than produced in-house, so it does not currently qualify), Kronaby
- **Citychamp Watch & Jewellery Group**: Eterna, Rotary, Rossini, Ebohr, Codex, Dreyfuss & Co., J&T Windmills
- **Timex Group**: Timex, TX
- **Mondaine Group**: Mondaine, Luminox
- **Independently owned but too large in scale**: Rolex, Tudor, Breitling, Patek Philippe, Audemars Piguet, Richard Mille, Seiko, Grand Seiko, Credor, Chopard (though Chopard-owned Ferdinand Berthoud is included), Chanel, Hermès, Citizen (though Citizen-owned Arnold & Son and Angelus are included), incl. Frédérique Constant, Alpina, Casio, Yema, Invicta, Spinnaker (owned by Dartmouth Brands)

**Exceptions — included despite group ownership**: Arnold & Son and Angelus (Citizen Group, via La Joux-Perret) and Ferdinand Berthoud (Chopard) are included because each operates as a genuinely autonomous manufacture — its own premises, its own team, full creative and technical control over its own movements and designs — functioning in practice like an independent house rather than a division of a larger commercial group.

This list is maintained on a best-effort basis and updates as brand ownership changes.

## Usage

Content may be cited with attribution to independentwatchmaking.com. The catalogue is updated regularly as new independent makers are added.
